intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by a white rabbit anchor and a single, struggling green shoot.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Critical Status: Only a single sprout remains; Pothos foliage is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in shared black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Single green sprout visible in early frames, showing signs of chlorosis and eventual collapse/desiccation.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments are present in the P3 pot, serving as a persistent, non-biological anomaly.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial presence of a single green sprout in P3.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Sprout shows signs of postural decline and loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Complete disappearance or total necrosis of the remaining sprout; the biome is now effectively barren.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total collapse. The lack of light (as noted in the world model) and potential over-saturation of the substrate have led to the death of all specimens. The 'Pothos' is effectively extinct in this environment.
anomalies: Total absence of registered flora (Systemic Failure)
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the botanical system. Starting from the earliest image, only one vestigial sprout was present in the P3 pot. Over the course of the five-day sequence, this sprout underwent rapid senescence and eventual disappearance. The yellow and black pots remain devoid of any vegetation, indicating that the registry is currently inaccurate to the physical reality of the desk. The environment is currently a sterile, non-productive substrate.
